display.purpose: Hero and page-defining headlines in Newsreader; emotional word goes italic + coral.

headline.purpose: Section and auth headings in Newsreader, scaled down.

title.purpose: Card titles, feature names, inline emphasis in Hanken Grotesk bold.

body.purpose: Default reading text in Hanken Grotesk; secondary copy uses Warm Muted.

label.purpose: Small UI labels and dividers; the only place uppercase tracking is allowed.

shadows.name: button-xs
shadows.purpose: Near-invisible hairline lift on outline buttons only. The system is otherwise flat.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default for button/input hover, focus, and color state changes (150–250ms band).

motion.name: rbfloat
motion.purpose: Decorative hero illustration float on the landing. Needs a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.

components.name: Primary Button (lg CTA)
components.description: The canonical call-to-action — coral fill, white text, used on the landing hero and auth forms.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 0 24px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)); color: var(--primary-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)) 90%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)) 50%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis nav action — transparent at rest, blush fill with coral text on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 0 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; background: transparent; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.2675 0.0113 36.49)); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: var(--accent, oklch(0.9525 0.0243 8.16)); color: var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form text field — hairline border, white fill over warm paper, coral focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { display: flex; width: 100%; height: 40px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid var(--input, oklch(0.922 0 0)); border-radius: 8px; background: #ffffff; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.2675 0.0113 36.49)); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.5058 0.0218 56.89)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, oklch(0.6907 0.2007 17.18)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring, oklch(0.6907 0.2007 17.18)) 50%, transparent); } .ds-input[aria-invalid='true'] { border-color: var(--destructive, oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--destructive, oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325)) 20%, transparent); }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: The landing's brand pill — blush fill, coral dot, coral bold label.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; height: 32px; padding: 0 16px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--accent, oklch(0.9525 0.0243 8.16)); color: var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-pill-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)); }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Landing feature tile — white fill, hairline border, soft 14px corners, no shadow (tonal lift).
components.html:
🥗

One tidy library

All your recipes in a single, searchable place — no more screenshots and scattered links.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; padding: 24px; border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.922 0 0)); border-radius: 14px; background: #ffffff; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-icon { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 56px; width: 56px; border-radius: 8px; background: #ffe9ec; font-size: 28px; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-weight: 700; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.2675 0.0113 36.49)); } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font-size: 14px; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.5058 0.0218 56.89)); }

components.name: Coral-Italic Headline
components.description: The signature headline treatment — Newsreader display in ink with one word pulled into coral italic.
components.html: Every recipe you love, finally in one cozy place.
components.css: .ds-headline { margin: 0; max-width: 18ch; font-family: Newsreader, ui-serif, Georgia, serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: clamp(2.25rem, 5vw, 3.875rem); line-height: 1.04; letter-spacing: -0.015em; text-wrap: balance; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.2675 0.0113 36.49)); } .ds-headline em { font-style: italic; color: var(--primary, oklch(0.691 0.201 17.2)); }

narrative.overview: recipebean looks and feels like a cherished physical recipe box that someone has kept for years — warm to the touch, a little personal, built to be opened a thousand times. The warmth is real but grown-up: it comes from a single confident coral, a Newsreader serif that handles the emotional beats in italic, and soft organic shapes — never from cute overload or a wall of pastel. The interface is calm and glanceable, the kind of thing you can read across a kitchen counter with flour on your hands. The system runs on a warm-paper canvas with near-black warm ink for text and a single saturated coral doing all the emotional and action work. Product surfaces stay restrained and tool-like; the marketing landing is allowed to bloom a little more, but both registers share the same paper, the same ink, the same one coral.
keyCharacteristics: One warm coral carries every action and emotional accent; everything else is paper, ink, and hairline.
Newsreader serif (often italic, often coral) for headlines and emotional words; Hanken Grotesk for everything functional.
Soft, generous radii (8–14px) and a warm-paper background — never stark white chrome.
Kitchen-legible first: large, high-contrast type beats density everywhere it counts.
Restrained in the app, a touch more expressive on the landing — same palette, different volume.

rules.name: The One Coral Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one accent color, and it is Coral. Pinks, peaches, and pastels are decorative imagery, not a second accent. If a screen needs to 'add color,' it adds coral or it adds nothing.

rules.name: The Muted-Floor Rule
rules.body: Warm Muted on Warm Paper is the system's contrast floor, and it is close to the AA line. Body and placeholder text must hit ≥4.5:1; never push secondary text lighter 'for elegance.' When in doubt, step toward Ink, not away from it.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Newsreader speaks; Hanken Grotesk works. Serif is for headlines and emotional words only — never buttons, labels, inputs, or data. A serif button is always wrong.

rules.name: The Coral-Italic Rule
rules.body: The signature flourish is a single word set in Newsreader italic and colored Coral inside an otherwise plain headline. Use it once per heading, on the word that carries the feeling — never on a whole line.

rules.name: The Tonal-Lift Rule
rules.body: A surface lifts by being whiter than the paper around it and ringed by one hairline — not by casting a shadow. If you reach for box-shadow to separate a card from the page, change the fill or the border instead.

dos: Do keep Coral as the only accent — actions, current selection, links, ratings, and the serif-italic flourish.
Do set headlines and emotional words in Newsreader (italic + coral for the signature beat) and everything operable in Hanken Grotesk.
Do lift surfaces with tone — Card White on Warm Paper plus one Hairline — not with shadows.
Do keep Warm Paper as the page background; reserve true white for cards and inputs.
Do verify Warm Muted text clears 4.5:1 on Warm Paper, and bump toward Ink whenever it's close.
Do prioritize kitchen-legibility: large, high-contrast ingredients and steps over dense layouts.
Do give every interactive element its full state set — default, hover, focus-visible (3px coral ring), active, disabled, error.
Do pair category colors with text labels; never encode meaning in color alone.
donts: Don't look like a cluttered recipe-blog SEO site — no ad-stuffed columns, no life-story-before-the-recipe.
Don't drift toward cold enterprise SaaS — no blue-gray dashboards, no dense corporate tables, no sterile chrome.
Don't reach for trendy AI-gradient startup moves — no glassmorphism, no purple gradients, no gradient text, no hero-metric template.
Don't go childish or over-cute — kawaii icons and pastels are seasoning, not the meal.
Don't set UI labels, buttons, inputs, or data in Newsreader. The serif never operates anything.
Don't color a whole headline line coral — the flourish is one word.
Don't add drop-shadows to cards to separate them from the page; change the fill or border instead.
Don't extend the identical icon+title+text feature-card grid on the landing — vary size, weight, or affordance.
Don't introduce a second accent color or a fourth named font; the system is one coral and two families.
